---
title: "Deploy with Terraform"
description: "Alternative IaC path â€” kept in sync with the CloudFormation template."
---

Lowkey ships a Terraform module at `deploy/terraform/`. It creates exactly the same AWS resources as the CloudFormation template (same VPC / IAM / EC2 / security services) so you can pick whichever IaC your team uses.

## Module layout

```
deploy/terraform/
â”śâ”€â”€ main.tf            # VPC, SG, IAM, EC2, security services
â”śâ”€â”€ variables.tf       # All input variables
â”śâ”€â”€ outputs.tf         # Instance id, stack info
â”śâ”€â”€ userdata.sh.tpl    # Same shape as the CFN template's UserData
â””â”€â”€ versions.tf        # Provider constraints
```

## Quick deploy

The lowkey installer runs Terraform for you when you pick "Terraform" as the deploy method:

```bash
curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
  --pack openclaw --profile builder \
  --method terraform
```

The installer calls `terraform init` â†’ `validate` â†’ `apply -auto-approve` under `deploy/terraform/`. If you want to review the plan before it runs, use the direct path below instead.

### Running Terraform directly

```bash
git clone https://github.com/inceptionstack/lowkey.git
cd lowkey/deploy/terraform

terraform init
terraform apply \
  -var="pack_name=openclaw" \
  -var="profile_name=builder" \
  -var="environment_name=my-openclaw"
```

This gives you the usual `plan` â†’ inspect â†’ `apply` loop with no auto-approve.

## Key variables

| Variable | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `pack_name` | string | `openclaw` | Which pack to install |
| `profile_name` | string | *(required)* | Which IAM profile. No default â€” **must be set**. Valid: `builder` / `account_assistant` / `personal_assistant`. |
| `environment_name` | string | *(required)* | Prefix for all resources |
| `instance_type` | string | `t4g.xlarge` | EC2 type (arm64) |
| `default_model` | string | per-pack | Default model ID |
| `model_mode` | string | `bedrock` | `bedrock` / `litellm` / `api-key` |
| `bedrock_region` | string | `us-east-1` | Bedrock region |
| `litellm_base_url` | string | `""` | LiteLLM proxy URL |
| `litellm_api_key` | string (sensitive) | `""` | LiteLLM API key |
| `litellm_model` | string | `claude-opus-4-6` | LiteLLM model name |
| `provider_api_key` | string (sensitive) | `""` | Direct Anthropic key |
| `kiro_from_secret` | string | `""` | Secrets Manager id/arn for Kiro API key |
| `existing_vpc_id` | string | `""` | Reuse existing VPC |
| `existing_subnet_id` | string | `""` | Public subnet in existing VPC |
| `enable_security_hub` | bool | true for builder/account_assistant | Turn on Security Hub |
| `enable_guardduty` | bool | same | Turn on GuardDuty |
| `enable_inspector` | bool | same | Turn on Inspector |
| `enable_access_analyzer` | bool | same | Turn on Access Analyzer |
| `enable_config_recorder` | bool | same | Turn on Config recorder |
| `repo_branch` | string | `main` | lowkey repo branch |

`variables.tf` has the authoritative set; check there for anything missing.

## Sensitive variables

`litellm_api_key` and `provider_api_key` are marked `sensitive = true`, so they won't print in plan / apply output. They **do** appear in Terraform state, so:

- Use a remote backend with encryption (S3 + KMS, Terraform Cloud, etc.).
- Prefer `kiro_from_secret` (stores a reference, not the key).

## Outputs

```bash
terraform output
```

Typical outputs: `instance_id`, `vpc_id`, `public_ip` (if enabled), `security_group_id`.

## Watching the deploy

The bootstrap script publishes progress to SSM Parameter Store regardless of IaC:

```bash
aws ssm get-parameter --name /loki/setup-status --query Parameter.Value
aws ssm get-parameter --name /loki/setup-step --query Parameter.Value
```

`IN_PROGRESS` â†’ `COMPLETE` â†’ you can SSM in.

## Tear-down

```bash
cd deploy/terraform
terraform destroy
```

Remove everything Lowkey created. Pre-existing VPCs (if passed via `existing_vpc_id`) are kept.

## Parity with CloudFormation

CFN and Terraform are kept in sync by the repo's tests (`tests/test-profiles.sh`, `tests/test-pack-contracts.sh`). If you spot a drift (a flag exists in one but not the other), it's a bug â€” please file an issue.
